query: Iran IAEA cooperation law suspension 2025 2026, current state of Iran nuclear inspections and diplomacy, US Iran nuclear deal negotiations status June July 2026
answer:
Research complete. Here's a summary of my key findings on the Iran-IAEA cooperation law and US-Iran nuclear diplomacy as of July 13, 2026:

## Bottom Line

**The situation is in acute crisis** — a ceasefire collapse and renewed war have overtaken the diplomatic track.

### IAEA Cooperation Law
- Iran's parliament passed the "Suspension of Cooperation with the IAEA" law **June 25, 2025** (221-1 vote); Pezeshkian signed it **July 2, 2025**. It **remains in force**.
- IAEA inspectors are still **barred from bombed enrichment sites** (Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan), limited only to Bushehr and the Tehran reactor.

### Inspections/Verification
- IAEA has had **~1 year of lost "continuity of knowledge."** It cannot verify Iran's last-declared stockpile of **9,874.9 kg enriched uranium, including 440.9 kg of 60% HEU**.
- June 10, 2026 Board resolution (21-3-10) reaffirmed Iran's **non-compliance**.

### US-Iran Diplomacy
- The **Islamabad Memorandum** (signed remotely June 17, 2026 by Trump and Pezeshkian) set a **60-day window** for a final deal.
- Talks in Switzerland (June 21-22) and Doha (July 1-2) made incremental progress but hit an impasse over inspections: Grossi says the MoU guarantees inspections; Iran says only after a final deal + full sanctions relief.

### Critical Recent Turn (the key headline)
- **July 8, 2026: Trump declared the ceasefire "over."** CENTCOM struck ~300 Iranian targets July 7-11 (no nuclear sites hit); Iran retaliated against six Gulf states and **closed the Strait of Hormuz**. Qatar/Pakistan are mediating to revive talks; the 60-day MoU clock is nominally still running (<40 days left) but talks are effectively frozen.

Full structured data (14 key facts, 12 data points, 16 sources) has been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool. Note: AskNews was unavailable (credit limit), so findings rely on web/Exa search, IAEA primary documents, ISIS analysis, and major news outlets.

sources.title: IAEA Board of Governors GOV/2026/33 - Implementation of NPT Safeguards Agreement in Iran
sources.snippet: Agency's estimate of Iran's total enriched uranium stockpile as of 13 June 2025 was 9874.9 kg... 440.9 kg enriched up to 60% U-235; loss of continuity of knowledge needs to be addressed with utmost urgency.

sources.title: IAEA Board Resolution GOV/2026/40 (adopted 10 June 2026)
sources.snippet: Resolution adopted with 21 in favour, 3 against and 10 abstentions; stresses Iran's Safeguards Agreement cannot be unilaterally amended or suspended.

sources.title: Iran president signs law suspending cooperation with IAEA
sources.snippet: President Masoud Pezeshkian signed a law suspending cooperation with the IAEA amid growing tensions.

sources.title: Iran's Suspension of IAEA Cooperation is a Political Gamble, not a Technical Decision
sources.snippet: On June 25, Iran's parliament approved 'Suspension of Cooperation with the IAEA' with 221 votes in favor and a lone abstention.

sources.title: 2025-2026 Iran-United States negotiations
sources.snippet: On June 17, 2026, Trump and Pezeshkian signed remotely the Islamabad Memorandum to end the war; three rounds of talks plus two rounds of technical talks.

sources.title: 2026 Iran war ceasefire
sources.snippet: On 17 June the presidents signed the Islamabad Memorandum establishing a 60-day period to negotiate final terms. On 8 July, Trump announced the ceasefire is over.

sources.title: 2026 Iran war
sources.snippet: On 8 July, the interim ceasefire collapsed after Iran allegedly struck commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz and the US responded by striking Iranian territory.

sources.title: UN nuclear chief says Iran inspections will happen, Tehran says after deal
sources.snippet: Grossi said the MoU stated 'explicitly' the agency would supervise inspections; Iran says access only after a final agreement and sanctions removal.

sources.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal grants access to Tehran's nuclear sites, UN watchdog says
sources.snippet: IAEA chief says interim peace agreement gives inspectors access to Iran's nuclear sites; MoU signed paving way for 60 days of talks.

sources.title: US-Iran talks in Doha: What were the outcomes and what's next?
sources.snippet: Next meeting after Khamenei funeral; Iran's parliament passed a law barring access, inspectors limited to Bushehr and Tehran reactor; $6bn frozen funds discussed.

sources.title: US conducts new wave of strikes on Iran as ceasefire falters
sources.snippet: Second night of strikes after Trump says ceasefire 'over' but suggests talks for lasting peace will continue.

sources.title: Iran War 2026 Daily Updates (Day 135, 12 July 2026)
sources.snippet: CENTCOM struck ~300 targets across three nights July 7-11; Iran fired widest single volley at six states; IRGC declared Strait of Hormuz closed; 60-day MoU window continues with under 40 days remaining.

sources.title: Analysis of IAEA Iran Verification and Monitoring and NPT Safeguards Reports - June 2026
sources.snippet: Near-total ongoing loss of monitoring due to Iran's suspension nearly one year ago; HEU location estimates ~265-287 kg at Isfahan, up to 80 kg at Fordow, 74-96 kg at Natanz.

sources.title: UN nuclear watchdog says it's unable to verify whether Iran has suspended all uranium enrichment
sources.snippet: IAEA says Iran has four declared enrichment facilities but has lost 'continuity of knowledge' about stockpiles.

sources.title: U.S., Iran agree on roadmap for final deal and plan to end military operations in Lebanon
sources.snippet: Progress in Switzerland talks toward a final deal within 60 days, including a mechanism to end hostilities in Lebanon.

sources.title: July 10, 2026 - Mediators work to de-escalate US-Iran tensions and revive talks
sources.snippet: Mediators working to de-escalate and get talks back on track after recent strikes; a lull in fighting amid diplomatic effort.

key_facts: Iran's parliament passed the 'Suspension of Cooperation with the IAEA' law on June 25, 2025 (221 votes in favor, 1 abstention); President Pezeshkian signed it into law July 2, 2025.
As of July 2026 the suspension law remains in force, barring IAEA access to bombed enrichment sites (Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan) and limiting inspectors to Bushehr power plant and Tehran reactor.
The IAEA has had no access to Iran's four declared enrichment facilities for nearly a year, resulting in loss of 'continuity of knowledge' over previously declared nuclear material.
Iran's last-verified enriched uranium stockpile (as of June 13, 2025) totaled 9,874.9 kg, including 440.9 kg of 60% HEU, 184.1 kg of 20%, 6,024.4 kg of 5%, and 2,391.1 kg of 2%.
Iran is the only NPT non-nuclear-weapon state to have produced and accumulated 60% HEU.
On June 10, 2026 the IAEA Board of Governors adopted a resolution (21 in favor, 3 against, 10 abstentions) reaffirming Iran's non-compliance with its Safeguards Agreement.
The Islamabad Memorandum was signed remotely on June 17, 2026 by Trump (at Versailles post-G7) and President Pezeshkian (in Tehran), establishing a 60-day window to negotiate a final deal.
Post-MoU talks: Switzerland/Lake Lucerne June 21-22, 2026 (agreed a 'roadmap'); Doha July 1-2, 2026 ('positive progress' but no breakthrough).

IAEA chief Grossi says the MoU explicitly provides for IAEA-supervised inspections; Iran (Deputy FM Gharibabadi) insists access to bombed sites comes only after a final deal and full sanctions termination.
On July 8, 2026 Trump declared the ceasefire 'over' after Iran allegedly struck commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
CENTCOM conducted three rounds of strikes on 300+ Iranian military targets during July 7-11, 2026 (no nuclear facilities were struck in this round).
On July 11-12, 2026 Iran launched its widest single missile/drone volley against six Gulf states, and the IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz 'closed until further notice'.
Qatar and Pakistan are mediating to de-escalate; the 60-day MoU negotiating window still nominally runs (under ~40 days remaining) but talks are effectively stalled amid open hostilities.
US waived Iranian oil sanctions for 60 days after the first post-MoU round of talks; $6 billion in frozen Iranian assets remains a negotiating point.

data_points.metric: Iran parliament vote to suspend IAEA cooperation (in favor)
Iran total enriched uranium stockpile (last verified)
IAEA Board resolution vote in favor (non-compliance reaffirmed)
Frozen Iranian assets discussed in talks
US oil sanctions waiver period after first post-MoU talks
Iranian military targets struck by CENTCOM July 7-11
Estimated HEU held in Isfahan/Esfahan tunnel complex
